results.possibleIdentity: Robert O'Brien (born 1939) is a British artist who was active in the late 1960s and 1970s and is noted for his background in theatre design and technology. He graduated in Theatre Design Technology from Central Saint Martins (the London Institute) in 1961. Following his graduation, he worked as a professional artist in London and Munich from 1964 to 1975, where he was associated with avant-garde venues such as the Grabowski Gallery. His training in theatre technology aligns with the interdisciplinary goals of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization founded in 1966 to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. In 1976, O'Brien moved to Hong Kong, eventually settling on Cheung Chau island, where he became a prominent figure in the local art scene. His work primarily explores landscape as a form of conceptual mapping, often utilizing a cubist or abstract style and incorporating modular elements that allow viewers to reconfigure the canvases. He has taught art and design at several institutions, including the Hong Kong Polytechnic University and the Hong Kong Academy for Performing Arts.
